meta.tagline: Following the internet's favorite German World Cup fan as he road-trips across the United States and Canada.
meta.disclaimer: Unofficial fan-made tracker based on public posts and published reporting.

current.headline: In Houston for Germany's opening World Cup match
current.detail: Freddy arrived after driving across the South and received a Houston welcome arranged by J.J. Watt.

nextStops.note: Reported invitation to meet Ella Langley at her concert.
Expected next World Cup destination for Germany's following match.

updates.title: J.J. Watt delivers a serious Houston welcome
updates.summary: A stay at the Post Oak Hotel, local food, and a personalized Texans jersey were among the surprises arranged for Freddy and his friends.

updates.title: Freddy reaches Houston
updates.summary: The group drove into downtown Houston late Saturday after the long trip across the South.

updates.title: An Ella Langley meetup is reportedly on the schedule
updates.summary: After her music became the soundtrack of the trip, Freddy said the group had been given an opportunity to meet Ella Langley at her June 18 Oklahoma City concert.

updates.title: New Orleans and Baton Rouge
updates.summary: Freddy's route continued through Louisiana before the final push west to Houston.

updates.title: The American road-trip checklist keeps growing
updates.summary: Buc-ee's, Bass Pro Shops, Raising Cane's, Walmart, Chili's, Waffle House, and other stops became part of the viral travel diary.

updates.title: Jordan-Hare Stadium becomes a major viral moment
updates.summary: Freddy attended Argentina vs. Iceland in Auburn and reacted to the stadium and eagle flyover with full European-mind-can-not-comprehend energy.

updates.title: The Waffle House review takes off
updates.summary: A late-night Waffle House visit in Georgia earned a 10/10 and became one of the trip's breakout posts.

updates.title: Six weeks, hand luggage, and mostly jerseys
updates.summary: Freddy announced the start of a six-week World Cup trip through the United States and Canada.
